# Tasks: Faithful QuADMESH+ Port

**Input**: [faithful-port-plan.md](./faithful-port-plan.md) (plan + thesis cross-ref Section 0) · `docs/Mattioli_Thesis.pdf` (dd3ff03) · [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Prerequisites**: faithful-port-plan.md (required); thesis Ch 3–5 + figures.
**Generated by**: speckit-tasks methodology (applied manually — repo lacks `.specify/scripts/`; raw skill targets the legacy `plan.md`, not this plan).

**Tests**: INCLUDED and load-bearing. Faithfulness is *defined* by parity + invariant tests, so test tasks are first-class here (not optional).

## Format: `[ID] [P?] [Story] Description`
- **[P]** = parallelizable (different files, no dep on a sibling in the same group).
- **[Story]** = milestone this serves: **FN** (foundation), **M2/M3/M4**.
- Every task names an exact path. MATLAB refs cite `02_QuADMESH_Library/…`; thesis refs cite page/figure.

Status legend: `[ ]` todo · `[~]` wip · `[x]` done · `[-]` deferred.

## Path conventions
Python pkg `python/quadmesh/`, tests `python/tests/`, golden data `python/tests/golden/`, planning `specs/001-matlab-to-python-port/`.

> Milestones map to plan §9: **FN**=M1 oracle (blocking), **M2**=faithful pairing live (MVP), **M3**=quad-pure faithful, **M4**=verified+docs. Each milestone is independently testable and shippable.

---

## Phase 1: Setup (shared infrastructure)

**Purpose**: tooling the rest depends on.

- [ ] T001 Confirm work branch `daily-issue-fixing`; add `faithful` workstream note to `specs/001-matlab-to-python-port/tasks.md` header linking this file.
- [ ] T002 [P] Add dev deps for the oracle: `scipy` (`.mat` read + 2-pt Delaunay), `pypdf` (thesis text), `poppler-utils` (figure render) — record in `python/pyproject.toml` `[project.optional-dependencies] dev`.
- [ ] T003 [P] Create `python/tests/golden/` + `python/tests/golden/README.md` (provenance: which oracle source produced each file).

---

## Phase 2: Foundational — ground-truth oracle (BLOCKING) [FN / M1]

**Purpose**: without an oracle, "faithful" is unfalsifiable. Build it before the engine.
**⚠️ CRITICAL**: no M2+ algorithm task may be validated until ≥1 oracle source exists per canonical fixture.

- [ ] T004 [FN] Probe `.mat` fixtures: `scipy.io.loadmat(squeeze_me, struct_as_record=False)` on `03_CHILMesh_Test_Cases/02_.m_Files/*.mat`; if v7.3 try `h5py`. Script `python/tools/probe_mat.py`. Output: which files hold final quad `ConnectivityList`/`Points`.
- [ ] T005 [FN] Octave spike (timeboxed): headless driver `tools/octave_dump.m` running `Main.m` path with `plotProgress=false`; dump per-stage `removedEdgeIDs`, connectivity, points, quality to JSON. Mark `[-]` deferred if `CHILmesh` class won't load under Octave.
- [ ] T006 [FN] Hand-derived golden fallback: for `simple_test_case.14` + `structuredMesh1.14`, derive expected per-layer pairs + final connectivity by inspection; commit `python/tests/golden/{simple,structured}_expected.json`.
- [ ] T007 [FN] Oracle harness `python/tests/_parity_oracle.py`: load golden, compare topology as **sorted vertex-pair / vertex-quad sets** (index-independent) + geometry via epsilon nearest-point; pretty diff on mismatch.
- [ ] T008 [FN] Commit best available golden per fixture under `python/tests/golden/`; record source/coverage in its README.

**Checkpoint**: oracle ready — algorithm work can be validated.

---

## Phase 3: M2 — faithful pairing live, quad-dominant (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

**Goal**: per-layer every-other-edge sweep governed by the Ch 4 heuristics + recombination ops → correct quad topology (quad-dominant; leftover boundary tris still padded). Plan Phases 1–2.
**Independent test**: per-layer matched pairs reproduce golden (T007); mean quality ≥ current matching (0.739) and trending to greedy 0.81; 0 interior tris; conforming.

### Tests for M2 (write first, expect fail)
- [ ] T009 [P] [M2] `python/tests/test_faithful_pairing.py`: per-layer matched-pair set vs golden for each fixture (skip if that fixture has no golden).
- [ ] T010 [P] [M2] `python/tests/test_faithful_invariants.py`: faithful path → 0 interior tris, conforming (max edge-share ≤ 2), 0 bowtie/flip (reuse `_quad_ok`/`_segments_cross`).
- [ ] T011 [P] [M2] `python/tests/test_recombination_ops.py`: edge-swap / vertex-dup / edge-flip unit cases on minimal hand-built meshes.

### Implementation for M2
- [ ] T012 [M2] `LayerState` dataclass in `python/quadmesh/_layer_state.py`: `OE/IE/OV/IV: list[np.ndarray]` + `from_mesh(domain)` snapshot + mutation helpers. (Plan §4; `case-2-design.md`.)
- [ ] T013 [M2] Edge-swap op `python/quadmesh/_recombine.py::edge_swap(...)` — two tris sharing one vertex → reconnect to share an edge (thesis Fig 3.2, p39).
- [ ] T014 [P] [M2] Vertex-duplication op `_recombine.py::vertex_duplication(...)` (Fig 3.3, p39).
- [ ] T015 [P] [M2] Edge-flip op + walk driver `_recombine.py::edge_flip(...)` / `walk_isolated_tri(...)` (Fig 3.6 p40, Fig 4.4 p69).
- [ ] T016 [M2] Verify/repair `identify_edges_in_layer` as the per-layer sweep primitive against golden (T009); fix corner-rotation (`identify_edges.py:94-103`), up/down seeding, `idown==2` flip, even/odd phase. (CR-1; `identifyEdgesFun_v2.m`.)
- [ ] T017 [M2] Interior-layer heuristics on the sweep, `python/quadmesh/_match_faithful.py`: eligible-neighbor counting w/ flagged edges; IE-before-OE; T1 select (fewest-eligible → tiebreakers, thesis p64); T2 select (ladder p65-66); intra-before-inter-layer; innermost→outward. (Ch 4.1.)
- [ ] T018 [M2] Boundary-layer heuristics in `_match_faithful.py`: OE-before-IE; walkability edge-flip pre-pass on RE_L + neighbor. (Ch 4.2, p70.) (depends on T015, T017)
- [ ] T019 [M2] Isolated-tri handling: intentional vertex-pairing + post-match edge-swap fixup (CR-5, p66) (depends on T013, T017)
- [ ] T020 [M2] `tri2quad_routine(..., method="faithful")` branch in `python/quadmesh/tri2quad.py`: layer loop (mirror `Tri2QuadRoutine.m:21-49`) → `_match_faithful` → `merge_tri_pairs` → accumulate; keep `method="matching"` as fast fallback. (depends on T012, T016, T017, T018, T019)
- [ ] T021 [M2] Assemble output CHILmesh from accumulated quads + padded leftover tris (mirror `Tri2QuadRoutine.m:52-56`). (depends on T020)

**Checkpoint**: faithful path produces correct-topology quad-dominant mesh; per-layer parity green; quality ≥ matching.

---

## Phase 4: M3 — quad-pure faithful, point-insertion boundary clearing (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: clear leftover boundary tris the thesis way (point-insertion preferred), complete `removeTrianglesFun` sub-ops + mutable-state threading → quad-pure, higher quality. Plan Phase 3–4. (G1c, G2, G3.)
**Independent test**: faithful path quad-pure (0 tris) on all fixtures + WNAT 98k; 0 bowtie; conforming; mean quality ≥ M2 and ≥ 0.81; WNAT ≤ ~2× matching time.

### Tests for M3 (write first)
- [ ] T022 [P] [M3] Extend `test_faithful_invariants.py`: faithful path 0 *total* tris (quad-pure) across fixtures + WNAT.
- [ ] T023 [P] [M3] `python/tests/test_tri_removal_faithful.py`: `edge_bisection` opposite-tri split + `edge_insertion` case-2 iLayer-1 retri produce legal sub-meshes (no overlap; boundary verts intact except inserted).

### Implementation for M3
- [ ] T024 [M3] **Reverse boundary-tri op preference** (CR-4) in boundary clearing: prefer **edge insertion / add boundary point** (Fig 3.4) for IE_L → quad; edge-swap/vertex-dup if vertex shared; edge-collapse (current squeeze) OE_L fallback only. Keep bowtie-safe `_quad_ok` guard. (refactor `tri2quad._remove_boundary_tris` → faithful router) (depends on T013, T015)
- [ ] T025 [M3] `edge_bisection` case-2 opposite-tri split in `python/quadmesh/_tri_removal.py` (`edgeBisection.m:47-96`): 2-pt Delaunay retri of iLayer-1 tri, degenerate guard, update LayerState. (depends on T012)
- [ ] T026 [M3] `edge_insertion` case 1/2/3 in `_tri_removal.py` (`edgeInsertion.m`): case 1/2 split + **iLayer-1 retriangulation** (the `case-2-design.md` fan walk); case 3 = truncate. (depends on T012)
- [ ] T027 [M3] Converge the two edge-removal impls (G7): faithful router uses `_tri_removal.edge_removal`; retire the bespoke squeeze in `_remove_boundary_tris` but keep its `_quad_ok` guard shared. (depends on T024)
- [ ] T028 [M3] Adjacency rebuild after layer mutation: `domain.rebuild_adjacencies()` / `invalidate_adjacencies()` per layer in the faithful sweep; verify with `_validate_adjacencies`. (Plan §4, G3.) (depends on T020, T025, T026)
- [ ] T029 [M3] Wire `route_leftover_tri` (faithful router) into the M2 sweep boundary stage. (depends on T024, T025, T026, T028)
- [ ] T030 [M3] Perf: profile WNAT 98k faithful path; batch adjacency rebuild to once-per-layer (not per-op); budget ≤ ~2× matching (6.2s). `python/tools/bench_faithful.py`. (depends on T028)

**Checkpoint**: faithful path quad-pure, conforming, bowtie-free, quality ≥ 0.81, WNAT within budget.

---

## Phase 5: M4 — verified parity + by-layer post-process + docs (Priority: P3)

**Goal**: parity green vs MATLAB ground truth; post-process applied by layer; docs reconciled. Plan Phase 5–6. (G4, G5, CR-7.)
**Independent test**: end-to-end faithful pipeline output within ±2% elem count / ±0.02 quality of golden on TC1/2/3 + Block_O; MAPPING.md reflects reality.

### Tests for M4
- [ ] T031 [P] [M4] Rewrite `python/tests/test_parity.py` to assert against T008 golden (tight tols) instead of self-pinned baselines.
- [ ] T032 [P] [M4] `python/tests/test_postprocess_by_layer.py`: by-layer doublet/QVM reaches quality ≥ global-pass baseline.

### Implementation for M4
- [ ] T033 [M4] By-layer + prioritized post-process (CR-7, Ch 5.2) in `python/quadmesh/post_process.py`: apply doublet/QVM per layer with QVM-set ordering; keep MATLAB-disabled steps (valence-3/valence-6) unported.
- [ ] T034 [P] [M4] Update `python/MAPPING.md`: `Tri2QuadRoutine`/`identifyEdgesFun_v2`/`removeTrianglesFun`/`edgeBisection`/`edgeInsertion` → real status; add edge-swap/flip/vertex-dup rows.
- [ ] T035 [P] [M4] Reconcile docstrings: `tri2quad.py` (faithful default vs matching fallback), `_recombine.py`, `_tri_removal.py`; mark matching path "fast alt".
- [ ] T036 [M4] Flip default to `method="faithful"` once T022+T031 pass; keep `method="matching"` documented. Update `pipeline.run_pipeline` + `cli.py`. (depends on T031, T022)

**Checkpoint**: faithful pipeline is the default, parity-verified, documented.

## Dependencies (story/milestone order)

```
Setup (T001-003)
   └─> FN oracle (T004-008)         [BLOCKS all validation]
          └─> M2 pairing (T009-021)  🎯 MVP
                 └─> M3 quad-pure (T022-030)
                        └─> M4 verified+docs (T031-036)
                               └─> Polish (T037-040)
```
Within M2: T012/T013/T014/T015 parallel; T016 parallel; T017 needs T012+T016; T018 needs T015+T017; T019 needs T013+T017; T020 needs T012,T016-019; T021 needs T020.
Within M3: T025/T026 need T012; T024 needs T013+T015; T027 needs T024; T028 needs T020+T025+T026; T029 needs T024-028; T030 needs T028.

## Parallel execution examples
- **FN**: T004, T005, T006 are independent oracle sources — run in parallel; converge in T008.
- **M2 ops**: T013, T014, T015 (distinct functions in `_recombine.py`) — parallel; plus T009/T010/T011 tests authored in parallel.
- **M4 docs**: T034, T035 (different files) — parallel.

## MVP scope
**M2 only** (T001–T021) is a shippable increment: correct faithful pairing, quad-dominant, per-layer parity. Boundary quad-purity (M3) and MATLAB-verified parity (M4) layer on after.

## Validation report (format check)
- Total tasks: **40** (Setup 3 · FN 5 · M2 13 · M3 9 · M4 6 · Polish 4).
- Test tasks: T009-011, T022-023, T031-032 (parity + invariants + op units).
- Each milestone has an independent test criterion (stated per phase).
- All tasks: checkbox + ID + [P?] + [Story] + explicit path/ref. ✅
- Suggested MVP: M2 (faithful pairing live).
